note: Structural-filter-first path (MULTIMODAL_TEXT_INTENT=on) is wired in orchestrator.py, not in test_retrieval_benchmark.py. The benchmark test calls retrieve_templates_with_scores directly, so env flag has no effect on hit@1 scores below. The table below measures embedding-only retrieval (identical with or without flag), as confirmed by two identical benchmark runs.

structural_filter_direct_measurement.description: filter_templates_by_intent called directly against persistent ChromaDB index after fix, broad pick_place intent (no count/feature constraints)

interpretation: At 8/321 coverage the structural filter operates on a tiny fraction of the corpus. Embedding-only hit@1 is unchanged because: (a) the benchmark prompts that target CP-01..05/09..11 already retrieve them via embedding alone; (b) the structural filter is only engaged by the orchestrator's MULTIMODAL_TEXT_INTENT path, not the benchmark test. The fix unblocks future Track B expansion: as more templates gain intent fields, the structural filter will engage and improve ranking precision. Without the fix, the filter was a guaranteed silent no-op on all production restarts.
what_this_unblocks: filter_templates_by_intent now returns correct candidates on persistent-index load (production-realistic path)
MULTIMODAL_TEXT_INTENT=on path in orchestrator.py now functions correctly on service restarts
Future intent field expansion to remaining 313 templates will immediately benefit retrieval quality
